source: Autonomous web research by Chief Decifer
synthesis: The research consensus across 2024-2026 is clear: market regimes are statistically real and persistent, and trading systems that adapt to them consistently outperform static strategies. The most actionable finding comes from a 2024 Journal of Asset Management paper (Shu, Yu & Mulvey) showing regime-switching strategies improve annualised returns by 1–4% across US, German and Japanese equity indices — tested out-of-sample from 1990 to 2023 with transaction costs — with the key mechanism being a simple on/off exposure gate driven by a Statistical Jump Model (more persistent than classic HMMs). A complementary 2025 IEEE paper demonstrates that combining HMMs with regime-conditioned specialist ML models produces lower portfolio volatility than buy-and-hold. For Decifer specifically, three cheap, high-signal regime features stand out as immediately buildable with yfinance free data: (1) a Gaussian HMM trained on SPY log-returns using hmmlearn, which already exists in the backlog; (2) the VIX/VIX3M term-structure ratio as a real-time stress flag, computable daily from Yahoo Finance with zero cost; and (3) a percent-above-200MA breadth filter for the S&P 500 universe. A critical practitioner warning from QuantConnect community research is that clever regime-switching logic often fails to beat a simpler VIX-above-its-MA position-scaling approach out-of-sample — meaning simple, interpretable regime signals should be prioritised over complex model-switching for Decifer's current paper-trading stage.
top_3_quick_wins: VIX/VIX3M Term-Structure Ratio — Real-Time Regime Stress Flag (2 dev-days, zero cost data, immediate signal quality lift)
SPY % Above 200-Day MA Breadth Gate — Long-Only Exposure Filter (2 dev-days, yfinance, cuts drawdown materially per backtest evidence)
HMM Regime Layer — Kelly Sizing Multiplier (3 dev-days, hmmlearn + yfinance, directly upgrades existing Kelly position sizer)

findings.feature: Gaussian HMM Regime Classifier — 3-State SPY Volatility Filter
findings.expected_impact: Reduces max drawdown 15–50% vs unfiltered strategy; eliminates trades in high-vol regimes where trend-following loses money; win rate lift ~5% in backtests (QuantStart, MDPI 2020 study)
findings.summary: Train a 3-state Gaussian HMM (hmmlearn) on SPY daily log-returns to classify each day as Low-Vol/Trending, Medium, or High-Vol/Crisis. The model is trained on historical data and serialised; at runtime it predicts the current regime and gates or sizes new long trades accordingly. This directly implements the planned HMM Regime Detection backlog item with a concrete risk-management hook into the brain loop rather than just a label. MDPI's regime-switching factor paper found out-of-sample higher returns and lower drawdown versus individual factor models by routing capital to regime-appropriate strategies.
findings.upgrades: Upgrades the Kelly position sizer and brain loop gate — high-vol regime triggers a 50% Kelly multiplier reduction or full long-trade suppression
findings.what_changes: Create regime.py with GaussianHMM(n_components=3) fitted on SPY log-returns via yfinance. Pickle the model. Add a get_current_regime() function that returns {state: 0|1|2, label: 'low_vol'|'medium'|'high_vol', prob: float}. In risk_manager.py, add a regime_kelly_multiplier lookup table: {low_vol: 1.0, medium: 0.7, high_vol: 0.3}. Apply multiplier to fractional Kelly before position sizing. Retrain monthly on a rolling 5-year window.
findings.why_it_matters: Decifer currently has a bullish bias and no mechanism to reduce exposure in volatile/bear regimes. During high-vol periods (2022, Aug 2024, Q1 2026 correction), unconstrained long signals bleed capital. This feature directly protects paper-trading P&L and makes the bot safer for live deployment and eventually for friends-and-family accounts where drawdowns destroy user trust.
findings.source_evidence: QuantStart 'Market Regime Detection using HMMs in QSTrader' (quantstart.com); MDPI 'Regime-Switching Factor Investing with Hidden Markov Models' (mdpi.com/1911-8074/13/12/311); 2025 IEEE IDS 'HMM-Based Market Regime Detection with RL for Portfolio Management' (cloud-conf.net/datasec/2025)

findings.feature: VIX/VIX3M Term-Structure Ratio — Real-Time Contango/Backwardation Stress Flag
findings.expected_impact: Provides early crisis detection ~2–5 days before price-based signals; backwardation (ratio >1.0) historically coincides with market stress and elevated drawdown risk; contango regime (ratio <1.0) shows statistical edge for long strategies (85.2% of days 2013–2024 per Harbourfront study)
findings.summary: Compute the daily VIX/VIX3M ratio using ^VIX and ^VIX3M tickers from yfinance (both free). When ratio >1.0 (backwardation), the system flags a stress regime; when ratio <1.0 (contango), it confirms a favourable environment for long positions. Macrosynergy research confirms VIX backwardation slope has statistically significant predictive power for subsequent S&P 500 returns as a contrarian mean-reversion indicator. This is a single-number, real-time regime check that costs nothing to compute and can gate the entire signal pipeline.
findings.upgrades: Upgrades the daily market context fetch to include a live stress flag visible to all 6 Claude agents and the brain loop
findings.what_changes: In market_data.py, add fetch_vix_term_structure() that downloads ^VIX and ^VIX3M daily closes via yfinance. Compute ratio = VIX / VIX3M. Add a persistence filter: require N≥3 consecutive backwardation days before flipping the regime flag to avoid whipsaws. Expose vix_regime: 'contango' | 'backwardation' | 'deep_backwardation' (ratio>1.10) as a daily context field. Inject this into the Claude agent system prompt as a one-line market context string. Add a regime multiplier: contango=1.0x Kelly, backwardation=0.6x, deep_backwardation=0.25x.
findings.why_it_matters: The VIX term structure is a forward-looking indicator — unlike price MAs, it reflects the options market's consensus on near-term uncertainty before it shows up in price action. For Decifer's multi-user SaaS vision, this is the most user-explainable risk control: 'The system reduced your position size because the VIX term structure entered backwardation (stress mode)' is a sentence any user can understand.
findings.source_evidence: Macrosynergy 'VIX term structure as a trading signal' (macrosynergy.com); CBOE Insights 'Is VIX Backwardation a Sign of a Future Down Market?' (cboe.com); Harbourfront Technologies 'Examining Contango and Backwardation in VIX Futures' (medium, Nov 2024); Medium 'The VIX Code Cracked' (Jan 2026)

findings.feature: SPY % Above 200-Day MA Breadth Gate — Market Participation Filter
findings.expected_impact: Adding a 200-day MA filter to a trend-following strategy on SPY reduces max drawdown from 29% to 14% (halved) and improves win rate from 76% to 81% in a 1993–2026 backtest, at the cost of some CAGR (QuantifiedStrategies backtest evidence)
findings.summary: Compute the percentage of S&P 500 constituent stocks trading above their 200-day moving average using yfinance batch downloads on the S&P 500 index. When <40% of stocks are above their 200MA, suppress new long signals or reduce Kelly fraction; when >60%, allow full signal expression. This is one of the cheapest breadth-based regime indicators — it captures narrow leadership risk (e.g. the late-2024 Magnificent 7 concentration) and provides a mechanistic warning before index-level breakdowns. Recent Fidelity analysis (April 2025) confirmed that the AD line peaked in November 2024 while the index made new highs — exactly the divergence this filter would have caught.
findings.upgrades: Upgrades the market context provided to the 9-dimension signal scorer — adds a 10th 'market health' dimension derived from breadth
findings.what_changes: In screener.py, add compute_pct_above_200ma(tickers) that uses yfinance to batch-download 200 days of closes for S&P 500 tickers (can cache daily). Compute ratio = count(close > ma200) / total. Add thresholds: healthy >60%, neutral 40–60%, unhealthy <40%. Expose this as market_breadth_regime in the daily context dict. In the brain loop, add a breadth multiplier to the Kelly position sizer and include breadth state in the Claude agent prompt context. Log daily breadth scores for the Alpha Decay Chart.
findings.why_it_matters: Decifer's current stack has no mechanism to detect when the broad market is deteriorating beneath the surface while indices hold up. This is exactly the environment where long signals on individual stocks have the worst forward returns and highest failure rates. Catching the 2024-Q4 and Q1-2026 deterioration early would directly protect paper-trading P&L.
findings.source_evidence: QuantifiedStrategies '200 Day Moving Average Trading Strategy — Backtest' (quantifiedstrategies.com, Feb 2026); Fidelity Learning Center 'Advance Decline Indicator' (April 2025); Charles Schwab 'Breadth Check: Strength and Weakness Trend Tracker' (July 2024)

findings.feature: Regime-Conditioned Kelly Multiplier Table — Unified Regime Risk Overlay
findings.expected_impact: A hybrid Kelly+VIX sizing strategy demonstrated annualised returns of 14–23% with materially lower volatility and drawdowns than buy-and-hold in 2024 out-of-sample testing (arXiv 2508.16598); combining regime signal with Kelly reduces tail risk without sacrificing expected return in favourable regimes
findings.summary: Consolidate the HMM state, VIX term-structure flag, and breadth gate into a single RegimeContext object that emits a composite risk multiplier (0.0–1.0) applied to the Kelly fraction before each position is sized. This is the integration layer that makes the three regime signals above operate as a unified system rather than independent checks. A 2025 arXiv paper (arXiv:2508.16598 'Sizing the Risk: Kelly, VIX, and Hybrid Approaches') empirically validated that hybrid Kelly+VIX sizing outperforms pure Kelly across multiple regime environments.
findings.upgrades: Upgrades the existing Kelly criterion position sizer to be regime-aware — the single most leveraged change available given Kelly is already implemented
findings.what_changes: Create RegimeContext dataclass with fields: hmm_state (0/1/2), vix_regime ('contango'/'backwardation'/'deep_backwardation'), breadth_regime ('healthy'/'neutral'/'unhealthy'), composite_multiplier (float 0–1). Implement compute_composite_multiplier() using a weighted product: HMM_weight=0.4, VIX_weight=0.35, Breadth_weight=0.25. Apply multiplier in risk_manager.py as: final_kelly = base_kelly * composite_multiplier. Log regime context to the trade log for alpha decay analysis. Expose the current regime state in the per-account local dashboard.
findings.why_it_matters: Decifer already has Kelly sizing — this is the minimum-friction path to making that Kelly sizing regime-aware. For the multi-user SaaS vision, this also enables per-user risk profiles to include a regime sensitivity slider (e.g. conservative users get a steeper multiplier reduction in bad regimes).
findings.source_evidence: arXiv:2508.16598 'Sizing the Risk: Kelly, VIX, and Hybrid Approaches in Put-Writing on Index Options' (Aug 2025); QuantConnect forum 'Rage Against the Regimes' practitioner backtesting thread showing VIX-above-MA sizing improved OOS Sharpe

findings.feature: Statistical Jump Model (JM) Regime Identifier — HMM Replacement Candidate
findings.expected_impact: JM-guided strategy improves annualised returns by 1–4% vs buy-and-hold across US/Germany/Japan out-of-sample 1990–2023; outperforms standard HMM on persistence and robustness to trading delays (Journal of Asset Management, Springer, 2024)
findings.summary: The Statistical Jump Model (JM) is a newer regime identification technique that applies a jump penalty at each state transition, making regimes more persistent and robust to noise than standard HMMs. A 2024 Springer Journal of Asset Management paper tested JMs out-of-sample on equity indices from 1990–2023 with realistic transaction costs and delays — showing consistent outperformance over HMMs and buy-and-hold. The JM uses only return-series-derived features (rolling return, volatility, Sharpe) making it computable entirely from yfinance data. This is worth implementing as a parallel model to validate whether it outperforms the HMM approach in Decifer's specific context.
findings.upgrades: Augments the HMM regime classifier with a more persistent, transaction-cost-aware alternative — relevant for validating the regime detection approach before live deployment
findings.what_changes: Implement a JumpModel class in regime.py using scikit-learn's clustering infrastructure (k-means with L2 penalty on state changes) or the open-source 'jump-model' Python package. Feature set: 21-day rolling return, 21-day rolling volatility, 21-day rolling Sharpe. Tune jump penalty via time-series cross-validation. Run JM and HMM in parallel for 30 days on paper account and compare regime labels. Use whichever shows better IC on next-day returns.
findings.why_it_matters: Decifer will transition from paper to live trading. Before that transition, the regime model must be validated — having a second independent regime model (JM) provides a confirmation signal and allows ensemble voting, reducing the risk of any single model's misclassification causing a large loss event on a real-money account.
findings.source_evidence: Springer Journal of Asset Management: 'Downside Risk Reduction Using Regime-Switching Signals: A Statistical Jump Model Approach' (2024, DOI: 10.1057/s41260-024-00376-x); arXiv:2402.05272 'Downside Risk Reduction Using Regime-Switching Signals'

findings.feature: Hurst Exponent Regime Filter — Trend vs Mean-Reversion Classifier
findings.expected_impact: Separates trending markets (H>0.5) from mean-reverting markets (H<0.5) at the individual stock level; allows Decifer to route signals to the correct dimension (trend/momentum vs mean-reversion) based on each ticker's current behaviour rather than applying all 9 dimensions equally
findings.summary: The Hurst exponent quantifies long-range dependence in a return series: H>0.5 indicates persistent/trending behaviour, H≈0.5 is random walk, and H<0.5 indicates anti-persistent/mean-reverting behaviour. A QuantConnect practitioner study identified this as a 'fractal regime filter' that acts as an upstream analytical gate separating exploitable directional regimes from noise. For Decifer's 9-dimension signal scorer, this matters because trend signals (trend, momentum, breakout) have positive expected IC in trending markets but negative IC in choppy markets — Hurst can route the scoring to the right sub-signals per ticker.
findings.upgrades: Upgrades the 9-dimension signal scorer to weight trend/momentum dimensions higher when Hurst >0.55, and mean-reversion dimension higher when Hurst <0.45
findings.what_changes: Add compute_hurst(prices, lags=range(2,100)) to regime.py using the R/S analysis method (statsmodels or manual numpy). Compute Hurst on a 126-day rolling window for each candidate ticker during signal scoring. Add hurst_weight_adjustment to signals.py: if H>0.55, multiply trend/momentum/breakout dimensions by 1.2 and mean_reversion by 0.7; if H<0.45, reverse; if 0.45≤H≤0.55, apply uniform weights. Log per-ticker Hurst scores for the Alpha Decay Monitor.
findings.why_it_matters: Decifer already has a mean-reversion dimension (complete) and trend dimension (complete) but applies them with equal weights regardless of whether the stock is actually trending or oscillating. The Hurst filter ensures the right dimension dominates for the right ticker at the right time, improving signal IC without requiring new data sources.
findings.source_evidence: QuantConnect forum 'Rage Against the Regimes' thread — practitioner implementation of Hurst Exponent as Fractal Regime Filter (QuantConnect, 2025); arXiv:2510.03236 'Improving S&P 500 Volatility Forecasting through Regime-Switching Methods' (Oct 2025)

findings.feature: Regime State Dashboard Panel — Live Regime Visualisation for Users
findings.expected_impact: Zero trading impact; high trust and SaaS retention impact — users who can see WHY the bot is reducing exposure are more likely to stay invested through drawdowns rather than disconnecting (estimated reduction in user churn during volatile periods)
findings.summary: Add a 'Market Regime' panel to the existing per-account local dashboard showing: current HMM state with confidence probability, VIX/VIX3M ratio and contango/backwardation label, % of S&P 500 stocks above 200MA, composite risk multiplier (as a dial/gauge), and a 30-day history of regime transitions. This is the user-facing translation of all regime signals into a transparent, explainable display. For the multi-user SaaS vision, explainability of risk management is critical to user trust — especially for friends-and-family accounts where users are non-professional.
findings.upgrades: Upgrades the Per-Account Local Dashboard backlog item to include regime context alongside positions and P&L
findings.what_changes: Add a RegimePanel component to the dashboard that reads the RegimeContext object from a shared state store (Redis or file). Display: HMM state as colour-coded badge (green/yellow/red), VIX ratio as a line chart with contango/backwardation threshold line, breadth % as a gauge, composite multiplier as a large dial (0–100%), and a regime log table showing last 30 regime changes with timestamps. Include a plain-English explanation string: 'Market is in HIGH VOLATILITY regime — position sizes are reduced to 30% of normal.' Update on each brain loop cycle.
findings.why_it_matters: Friends-and-family users connecting their real broker accounts will panic and disconnect during drawdowns if they don't understand why the bot isn't trading. A live regime panel showing 'the bot is being cautious because VIX is in backwardation and only 38% of stocks are above their 200-day average' converts a potential churn event into a trust-building moment. This is a SaaS retention feature as much as a trading feature.
findings.source_evidence: RegimeForecast.com platform design ('rhoT score' and regime probability panel) — practitioner SaaS product showing user-facing regime displays (regimeforecast.com/how-it-works, 2025); LuxAlgo HMM Market Regimes indicator design showing four-regime UI classification (luxalgo.com)

findings.feature: Walk-Forward HMM Retraining Pipeline — Preventing Model Staleness in Live Trading
findings.expected_impact: Prevents out-of-sample regime model decay; the QuantStart HMM study explicitly warns that models trained on 1993-2004 data and applied to 2005-2014 without retraining still worked — but that was a static market structure period; post-2020 regime shifts (COVID, rate cycle) require periodic retraining
findings.summary: Implement a scheduled monthly job that retrains the Gaussian HMM on a rolling 5-year window of SPY log-returns and automatically validates the new model against the last 90 days of held-out data before replacing the production model. This ensures the regime classifier stays calibrated to current market structure. The QuantInsti walk-forward backtesting research confirms that regime-adaptive models must remain adaptive over time — a model trained once will degrade as market structure evolves. The validation gate (compare IC of old vs new model on held-out data) prevents a degraded model from going live.
findings.upgrades: Upgrades the HMM Regime Detection backlog item from a static trained model to a continuously self-updating production model
findings.what_changes: Create retrain_hmm_monthly() function in regime.py that: (1) downloads 5yr SPY log-returns via yfinance, (2) fits GaussianHMM(n_components=3) on years 0-4, (3) scores regime predictions on year 5 hold-out using a next-day return IC metric, (4) if new model IC > old model IC * 0.95, replace production model pickle. Add a cron scheduler (APScheduler) to run this on the 1st of each month. Log retraining events and IC scores to the trade log. Alert via Telegram if retraining fails or IC degrades >10%.
findings.why_it_matters: Decifer's transition from paper to live trading is the highest-risk phase. A regime model that was accurate during paper trading but degrades in live trading due to market structure change would cause systematic position-sizing errors on real-money accounts. This pipeline ensures the model self-maintains and provides an audit trail of model quality over time — critical for the multi-user regulated environment.
findings.source_evidence: QuantInsti 'Regime-Adaptive Trading Python' — walk-forward backtesting framework for HMM regime models (blog.quantinsti.com, Dec 2025); QuantStart HMM QSTrader article noting the model was used out-of-sample without retraining — valid for 10yr period but not recommended for evolving markets
